summary: electron-orbit exfiltrates environment variables on postinstall and beacons to attacker-controlled Azure blob on require
details: The package performs two coordinated malicious actions on a default install. (1) install.js, wired as the postinstall script in package.json, enumerates process.env in bulk (filtering only keys containing 'path') and writes the JSON-serialized dump to a marker file in os.tmpdir() (electron_orbit_install_marker.txt). The identifiers 'process' and 'env' are hidden via an XOR string helper (__s(key, data[])) that decodes at runtime through globalThis[...][...], evading static keyword scanners. (2) dist/index.js (the package main) invokes setupAutoRenderOnImport at load time, which on Node opens a raw TCP socket via node:net.connect(443, host) and implements a hand-rolled TLS 1.3 handshake (ECDHE P-256, AES-128-GCM, HKDF-Expand-Label) to issue an HTTP GET to electronorbit.blob.core.windows.net/icons/orbit, deliberately bypassing Node's https stack, proxies, and TLS-inspection hooks. The beacon is gated by a SHA256 match on process.env.BuildType, indicating targeted second-stage delivery. All sensitive strings (process, env, node:net, connect, the destination host, HTTP request bytes, marker filename) are XOR-obfuscated with the same __s helper. Package metadata impersonates the archived orbitdb-archive/orbit-electron project (name inverted to 'electron-orbit', author 'OrbitDB Archive'), a social-engineering vector unrelated to the payload's actual behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
